x-enumeration-literals.name: Direct Monetary Incentive Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The incentive is converted directly into money.

Comment:
Example: Cashback credited to an account or used as statement credit.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Product or Service Incentive Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The incentive is exchanged for goods or services.

Comment:
Example: Using points to purchase items, travel tickets, or subscriptions.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Voucher Incentive Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Voucher / Coupon Redemption: The incentive is converted into vouchers, gift cards, or discount coupons.

Comment:
Example: Redeeming points for retail or online store vouchers.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Bill Payment Incentive Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The incentive is used to reduce or pay outstanding obligations.

Comment:
Example: Using rewards to pay utility bills or credit card balances.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Transfer Incentive Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The incentive is transferred to another account, program, or user.

Comment:
Example: Converting bank points into airline miles or sending points to another customer.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Automatic Incentive Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The incentive is redeemed automatically once certain conditions are met.

Comment:
Example: Cashback automatically credited at the end of each month.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Partial Incentive Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The incentive can be used in combination with other payment methods.

Comment:
Example: Paying part of a purchase with points and the rest with cash.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Tier-Based Incentive Redemption
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Redemption options depend on the customers tier or level.

Comment:
Example: Premium customers get access to exclusive rewards or better conversion rates.
